entry.title: Re: How to know about Licenses from SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.
entry.content:
Hi @aayushsinha 

very helpful Blog 🙂

Btw. have you heard about the following circumstances, which one of my customers came across...?

If one SAC tenant has been ordered with only Planning Users (Prof. & Standard) and then the customer decides to cancel them and order only BI Reporting Users ... will they automatically get a new SAC tenant or is this possible in the same one? (I know that planning models cannot be changed etc. with BI roles, but this is not the topic right now).

For my feeling a new tenant might only be relevant if the old was Neo and it should be switched to CF, or if the tenant was maybe on private option and BI reporting cannot remain in it, but must be a public tenant? Or was there maybe just an error during the cancellation and new order process?

I did not find any information about this in the SAC Help pages... do you have a link about this?

Any feedback appreciated. Thanks a lot.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: Unlocking the True Potential of Data in Files with SAP HANA Database SQL on Files in SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In today's data-driven world, enterprises collect more data than ever before. Unlocking the potential hidden insights within this data, while seamlessly connecting the dots among both SAP and non-SAP applications, is not only essential for adapting to an ever-changing business environment but also crucial for differentiating your business from competitors.

Imagine having a treasure trove of business data stored in common formats like CSV, Parquet, and Delta table (Open Table Format). Each holds a vast volume of valuable information. Starting with SAP HANA Cloud QRC 03/2024, I'm thrilled to announce that SAP HANA Database offers native SQL on Files capabilities. This unlocks these chests with powerful analytical query processing capabilities, like what has been done with SAP HANA Database before. This enhancement makes your structured data in files instantly accessible and incredibly powerful while leveraging all the existing capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud.

This new native SQL on Files capability allows you to directly access and read data in SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files, especially in CSV, Parquet, and Delta table (Open Table Format), without moving or loading them into SAP HANA Database. This will also enable better control of the cost-performance balance for the workload, with increased flexibility. In a nutshell, this new capability includes the ability to:

  • Link your SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database to SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files with one click in SAP HANA Cloud Central (only available in multi-environment edition).
  • Access data in files stored in SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files on-the-fly by creating read-only virtual tables that point to the files or directory, and then executing SQL queries.
  • Benefit from support for file formats such as CSV, Parquet, and Delta table (Delta table is an Open Table Format table based on Parquet files). Since Delta table provides table semantics, it is the preferred choice for query processing.
  • Execute time travel queries on Delta table by specifying the version or timestamp in the SELECT statement.

 

Prerequisites and considerations

Please ensure that all the following prerequisites are met before following the steps in this blog.

  • Both SAP HANA Database and data lake Files are provisioned and running in SAP HANA Cloud. Please refer to Provisioning a SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database and Adding a Standalone Data Lake to SAP HANA Cloud (creating a data lake instance with the data lake Relational Engine disabled will provision a file-based data lake).
  • SAP HANA Database and data lake Files should be in the same subaccount.
  • The version of the SAP HANA Database in SAP HANA Cloud is QRC 03/2024 or later.
  • Files are already stored in the data lake Files using hdlfscli. If not, please refer to the Getting Started with Data Lake Files HDLFSCLI guide and upload the files.

When uploading files, if both Parquet and Delta Lake formats are used, it is highly recommended to separate directories because both formats basically use the same file extension (.parquet), while Delta Lake has an additional _delta_log folder for the transaction log.

 

Getting started with SAP HANA Database Native SQL on Files in SAP HANA Cloud

If the above conditions are met and the files are ready in data lake Files, let's configure the connection between the SAP HANA Database and the data lake Files with a simple one-click process in SAP HANA Cloud Central.

Link your SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database to SAP HANA Cloud, data lake Files:

If you have provisioned the SAP HANA Database and data lake Files in SAP HANA Cloud, you can see both in SAP HANA Cloud Central. To use SQL on Files, a secure connection between the SAP HANA Database and data lake Files should be created. For this, if you click on your SAP HANA Database in SAP HANA Cloud, you will now see a new tab named Linked Data Lake Files, which lists all the Data Lake Files instances in the same subaccount, as shown below. Please note that this feature is only available in the multi-environment edition of SAP HANA Cloud Central. To use the multi-environment edition, please follow the instruction in Subscribing to the SAP HANA Cloud Administration Tools.

In this screen, all you need to do is simply click Link, and it will handle everything, including certificates, trust, authorization, and remote source creation.

With this one click, as you can see in the example below, my demo-hdb is linked to demo-hdlf via an auto-generated remote source called demo-hdlf_rs.

You can also unlink and delete the auto-generated remote source by simply clicking Unlink. However, it will drop the remote source and all objects such as virtual tables that depend on it in the SAP HANA Database. Even though this action will not change anything in your data lake Files, careful operation is required as it cannot be undone. And if you link data lake Files via SAP HANA Cloud Central, please use unlink and do not drop the auto-generated remote source manually.

Please also refer to the links below for further details.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create Links to Data Lake Files Instances

Alternatively, the simple one-click process described above can also be achieved through SQLs and manual configurations. However, since using the one-click SAP HANA Cloud Central way is the recommended method, I will not address this alternative method in this blog. If you're interested in this alternative method, please refer to the links below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Set Up Certificates for Linking to Data Lake Files
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create an SQL on Files Remote Source

Create virtual tables:

Once you link the SAP HANA Database to data lake Files, virtual tables can be created by pointing to files stored in the data lake Files. All examples in this blog are based on TLC Trip Record Data.

Let's start with a simple CSV file. To create a virtual table by pointing to a CSV file, you can use the CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE statement. In this case, column names and data types should be defined as in the example below.

-- create a virtual table by pointing to a CSV file stored in data lake Files
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.TAXI_ZONE (
    "LocationID" INT PRIMARY KEY,
    "Borough" NVARCHAR(20),
    "Zone" NVARCHAR(50),
    "service_zone" NVARCHAR(20)
    ) AT "demo-hdlf_rs"."/csv/" AS CSV;

In this case, you should make sure that the CSV file does not have column headers, and the defined data type should be large enough to read the data. Otherwise, you will see the "too large value for column" error when executing a query on the virtual table, even though the virtual table is successfully created.

Please also find the example result in the screenshot below.

In SQL on Files, you can also specify the file name (e.g., /csv/taxi_zone_lookup.csv) instead of just the directory (/csv/). Using the directory allows for the automatic detection of any additionally uploaded files, while using the specific file name will not auto-detect any additionally uploaded files.

Now, let’s exclude the second column from the virtual table. Please see the below example.

-- create a virtual table by pointing to a CSV file without second column
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.TAXI_ZONE_NO_BOROUGH (
    "LocationID" INT PRIMARY KEY,
    "Zone" NVARCHAR(50),
    "service_zone" NVARCHAR(20)
    ) AT "demo-hdlf_rs"."/csv/" AS CSV FILE COLUMN LIST(1, 3, 4);

Please also find the example result in the screenshot below.

Here, as you can see, the second column Borough is excluded by using FILE COLUMN LIST(1, 3, 4), which means that only the first, third, and fourth columns are referred to when creating a virtual table.

Alright, now let’s see a Parquet example. For Parquet and Delta table, you can use GET_REMOTE_SOURCE_FILE COLUMNS built-in procedure to retrieve column information. Please note that this built-in procedure cannot be used for CSV files.

-- retrieve column information (parquet, delta table only)
CALL GET_REMOTE_SOURCE_FILE_COLUMNS ('demo-hdlf_rs', '/parquet/', 'PARQUET');

Please also find the example result in the screenshot below.

As you can see, you can find the recommended data types for each column in the SQL_DATA_TYPE. For more information, please refer to the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: GET_REMOTE_SOURCE_FILE_COLUMNS

With this information, I can easily write a CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE statement like the one below.

-- create a virtual table by pointing to Parquet files
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.YELLOW_TRIP (
    "VendorID" INT,
    "tpep_pickup_datetime" TIMESTAMP,
    "tpep_dropoff_datetime" TIMESTAMP,
    "passenger_count" BIGINT,
    "trip_distance" DOUBLE,
    "RatecodeID" BIGINT,
    "store_and_fwd_flag" NVARCHAR(1),
    "PULocationID" INT,
    "DOLocationID" INT,
    "payment_type" BIGINT,
    "fare_amount" DOUBLE,
    "extra" DOUBLE,
    "mta_tax" DOUBLE,
    "tip_amount" DOUBLE,
    "tolls_amount" DOUBLE,
    "improvement_surcharge" DOUBLE,
    "total_amount" DOUBLE,
    "congestion_surcharge" DOUBLE,
    "Airport_fee" DOUBLE
    ) AT "demo-hdlf_rs"."/parquet/" AS PARQUET;

In this example, since I know the store_and_fwd_flag column only has Y or N, I set this as NVARCHAR(1).
Please also find the example result in the screenshot below.

If needed, FILE COLUMN LIST can also be used for Parquet, as shown in the example below.

-- create a virtual table by pointing to Parquet files with 3 columns
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.YELLOW_TRIP (
    "VendorID" INT,
    "congestion_surcharge" DOUBLE,
    "Airport_fee" DOUBLE
    ) AT "demo-hdlf_rs"."/parquet/" AS PARQUET FILE COLUMN LIST(1, 18, 19);

For CSV and Parquet, directory-based partitioning is supported for better performance by pruning unnecessary files during query execution. For more information, please refer to the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Create a Virtual Table

Last but not least, let's see a Delta table example. For this example, the Parquet files shown above are converted into a Delta table. Also, the timestampNtz data types are converted to timestamp because currently, Reader Version 1 is the only version supported in SQL on Files.

For reading a Delta table, the entire Delta table path should be uploaded. Please also refer to the Converting from Parquet to Delta Lake. Different from CSV and Parquet, a virtual table can be created without defining the columns and corresponding data types, as shown in the example below.

-- create a virtual table by pointing to a delta table
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE DEMO.YELLOW_TRIP_DELTA AT "demo-hdlf_rs"."/delta/" AS DELTA;

If necessary, you can also define data types for each column, but different from CSV or Parquet, the column name must be the same as the Delta table column name. Additionally, FILE COLUMN LIST and directory-based partitioning are not supported with a Delta table.

For a Delta table, time travel queries are supported, as shown in the examples below.

-- the latest version
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM DEMO.YELLOW_TRIP_DELTA;

-- version 1
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM DEMO.YELLOW_TRIP_DELTA FOR VERSION AS OF '1';

In addition to the version (FOR VERSION AS OF), a timestamp (FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF) can also be used. For more information, please refer to the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Time Travel Queries on Delta Tables

Additionally, you can check the latest version of the Delta table with the DELTA_LAKE_TABLE_VERSION built-in procedure and retrieve the change data feed between given versions of a Delta table with the DELTA_LAKE_TABLE_CHANGES built-in procedure. However, to use the DELTA_LAKE_TABLE_CHANGES built-in procedure, the enableChangeDataFeed must be set for the Delta table. Please also refer to the links below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: DELTA_LAKE_TABLE_VERISON
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: DELTA_LAKE_TABLE_CHANGES

 

Recommendations and supported data types

Now that you have understood the basic operations of using the SAP HANA Database Native SQL on Files capability in SAP HANA Cloud, please carefully review the recommendations and supported data types provided in the links below before proceeding further.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Recommendations
  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Supported Data Types

 

Monitoring, performance analysis, and trouble shooting

For monitoring, there are various system views available to monitor SQL on Files operations. Please refer to the link provided below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: System Views for SQL on Files Monitoring

For further performance analysis, including EXPLAIN PLAN and SQL Analyzer Tool for SAP HANA, please refer to the detailed information provided in the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Performance Analysis

For troubleshooting, traces can be used to address issues. Please also find information on how to set trace levels in the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Troubleshooting

 

FAQs

Q: To use this SQL on Files feature, do I need to subscribe to any additional service or pay more?
A: No, all you need is a right-sized SAP HANA Database and data lake Files in SAP HANA Cloud. It requires proper sizing of memory and computing for the SAP HANA Database, as well as the amount of data read from the system during a specific month (API calls) of data lake files. Please refer to the SAP HANA Cloud Capacity Unit Estimator.

Q: Are there any limitations on the file size or volume that SQL on Files can handle?
A: There is no hard limitation, but it is limited by the provisioned SAP HANA Database size in SAP HANA Cloud. If the required resources to execute an SQL on Files query are higher than the provisioned SAP HANA Database size, such queries cannot be executed and scaling up of SAP HANA Database should be considered. To overcome the limitation of a single SAP HANA Database instance, we have a plan to support elastic compute nodes in the future.

Q: This SQL on Files feature is read-only from the SAP HANA Cloud Database perspective. If I want to execute DMLs on a Delta table, how can I do this?
A: Since Apache Spark can be used with the data lake Files, DMLs are expected to be executed separately via Apache Spark. Please refer to the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Data Lake User Guide for Data Lake Files: Data Lake Files with Apache Spark

Q: It seems that it shares the same virtual table or federation framework as SAP HANA smart data access (SDA). Does this mean it also supports toggling to replica tables or creating a view on it for static result cache?
A: Yes, toggling between virtual tables and replica tables is supported, but only snapshot replicas are supported. Please refer to the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Toggle Between Virtual Tables and Replica Tables

Creating a view on a virtual table and then creating a static result cache on the view is also supported.

Q: Is it recommended to create statistics for better performance?
A: SQL on Files virtual tables offer runtime data statistics collection, so you don't need to explicitly create statistics with the CREATE STATISTICS statement since this process happens automatically. Please refer to the link below.

  • SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA Database SQL on Files Guide: Statistics

 

Conclusion

In conclusion, the new SAP HANA Database Native SQL on Files capability in SAP HANA Cloud marks a significant advancement in unlocking the potential of data within different file formats. This enhancement not only provides instant access to valuable business data in files but also seamlessly integrates them with existing data and capabilities in SAP HANA Database.

With this direct and instant access to data lake Files in SAP HANA Cloud without ingestion, it will offer new possibilities for enterprises seeking to leverage their data for strategic decision-making, while enabling a modern data analytics and data engineering ecosystem.

Continuously, we are planning for further enhancements and additional features in the future, such as Delta Sharing support and access to external object storages. Please visit our SAP Road Map Explorer to stay tuned for the upcoming updates.

entry.title: Connecting the Facts: SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine for Business Context
entry.content:
Introduction:

In Q1 2025, SAP is planning to release the knowledge graph engine as a new capability for SAP HANA Cloud platform. This new multi-model engine allows businesses to connect and analyze information more effectively. SAP HANA Cloud will enable smarter applications and improve user experiences by using data stored in a graph to answer complex questions.

Knowledge graphs connect corporate knowledge, which makes them especially beneficial for powering large language models (LLMs) and generative AI capabilities. Developers can use business context to create applications that provide more precise and relevant answers, enhancing user interactions and improving business processes.

For many, the topic of knowledge graphs is new and exciting. I share below a brief introduction on the major concepts and how SAP plans for those concepts to materialize in SAP HANA Cloud.

Ontologies

An ontology is a guiding framework used to define at a higher level (i.e. classes, categories, groupings, etc.) the facts that occur in a domain and how those higher-level descriptions should relate to each other. Instead of focusing on the facts, an ontology regulates the resulting knowledge graph. For example, in the context of movies, an ontology could define the concept of "movies" and its attributes like title, director, genre, and release date, making the framework flexible and scalable for future data. Ontologies act as the blueprint for knowledge graphs, structuring and connecting data to allow deeper insights and reasoning.

Ontologies are critical for certain use cases where deep, structured relationships are required. For example, semantic networks often represent ontologies to map out concepts and relationships, and these structures can help with reasoning and inference. However, not every knowledge graph requires a complex ontology from the start. As needs grow, ontologies can be added to enrich the graph. The flexibility of knowledge graphs allows one to start small, deliver value quickly and expand over time as use cases become more complex.

Movies Ontology

Knowledge Graph

A knowledge graph is a structured representation of data (adhering to ontology rules), enhanced by real-world information. The knowledge graph captures relationships between entities, such as people, products, or events, and connects them in a meaningful way. Unlike data in normalized relations, a knowledge graph organizes information as triples where facts are represented as subject, predicate, and object. These triples form the core of the knowledge graph, revealing the logical relationships between different entities.

For example, consider the above example of movies ontology. A knowledge graph can store the facts of the movie Jurassic Park into a tripes format: Jurassic Park (subject) is directed by (predicate) Steven Spielberg (object). When you add more details, like the year of release, production company, and the sequel information, you can see the formation of a knowledge graph that connects all these data points.

 

Knowledge graphs are set apart by their ability to traverse relationships, much like navigating through a web of interconnected facts. Like reading through an encyclopedia, each piece of knowledge leads to another, revealing a chain of actionable insights. This search ability is especially powerful for tasks requiring complex reasoning, such as analyzing a supply chain. A virtual supply chain representation is a great example of connecting independent data silos to create knowledge. A company can trace the origin of materials, understand how external factors like weather disruptions in a supplier's region might impact production, and identify alternative suppliers with more sustainable practices. Thus, the knowledge graphs can help businesses mitigate potential risks and optimize decision-making in real time.

Movies Knowledge Graph

SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine Planned Features

With the upcoming knowledge graph engine, SAP HANA Cloud gains the ability to manage and query semantically connected relationships – all within the same database as other relational and multi-model engines.

The key highlights are:

  1. Native RDF Triple Store: One of the key features of the SAP HANA Cloud knowledge graph engine is its native support for RDF triple store. RDF (Resource Description Framework) is a widely accepted standard for modeling and representing data in graph structures. With this support, the platform allows users to store and manage ‘subject-predicate-object’ triples directly, providing an efficient and scalable way to represent relationships between data entities. This native capability ensures that the data stored in the knowledge graph adheres to global standards for graph databases, enabling seamless and deeper insights from complex relationships.
  2. SQL and SPARQL Interoperability: The knowledge graph engine will support SPARQL, the specialized query language for knowledge graph data, while tightly integrating it with SQL. This is achieved with the introduction of the following new SQL constructs:
    • SPARQL_TABLE (): A SQL table function that allows developers to embed SPARQL SELECT queries within SQL statements, returning results in a table format that can be processed further using SQL operations like filters, joins, and aggregations.  
    • SPARQL_EXECUTE (): A Stored procedure that provides an interface for executing all types of SPARQL statements, including both SPARQL Query Forms and SPARQL Update.
    • SQL_TABLE (): A SPARQL function that allows users to embed SQL queries directly within SPARQL statements. This function federates queries to the SQL engine (HANA index server process), with the results of the SQL statement being converted into graph patterns. SQL projections are mapped to corresponding SPARQL variables, enabling smooth integration between SQL and graph data.

SAP HANA Cloud knowledge graph engine vs the SAP HANA Cloud graph engine

SAP HANA Cloud already offers a graph engine designed to handle property graphs, which are excellent for analyzing relationships between entities. Now let’s use a retail supply chain use case as an example to highlight the differences between property graphs and knowledge graphs.

Property Graphs: These graphs are ideal for analyzing relationships between entities. In a property graph, data is represented as nodes (entities) and edges (relationships), where each edge can hold different properties that describe the connection between nodes. For instance, in a retail scenario, a property graph might show that Supplier A "delivers" Product X to Store Y. Another edge might indicate that Supplier B also delivers Product X to Store Z. This structure focuses on the relationships between entities, making it easy to map out who supplies what to whom, and where the products are sold.

Knowledge Graphs: In contrast, knowledge graphs focus on establishing facts and the logical relationships between those facts. They use a subject-predicate-object structure (triples), where each fact is stored as a relationship. For example, in a supply chain context, a knowledge graph captures facts such as "Product A is made from recycled materials," "Supplier X follows sustainable sourcing practices," and "Store Y is located in an eco-friendly area." This type of graph goes beyond just showing who supplies the product, it allows you to ask more sophisticated questions, like "Which suppliers with eco-friendly practices are providing products to stores located in green-certified regions?"

In short, while property graphs help you visualize and analyze relationships between different entities, knowledge graphs bring context and reasoning into the mix, making it easier to derive actionable insights. Together, both graphs give you a complete picture of how entities interact and the deeper implications of those relationships.

Use Cases:

  1. Enhance your Intelligent Data Applications & Gen AI:  The in-memory database capabilities of SAP HANA Cloud already provide high-performance data processing, but the addition of a knowledge graph enables applications to gain an extra layer of understanding. This new feature is particularly valuable when it comes to enabling Gen AI use cases, such as grounding large language models (also called GraphRAG) in domain-specific knowledge. By storing and retrieving semantic facts about a business's operations, relationships, and data, the knowledge graph can provide LLMs with the context they need to deliver more accurate and relevant responses - ultimately improving the quality of human-computer interaction.
  2. Establish a Data Fabric: With the Knowledge Graph Engine working seamlessly alongside the other multi-model engines in SAP HANA Cloud, such as the relational data, spatial, graph, JSON document store and vector engines, businesses can now bring structured and unstructured data together in a single platform, establishing a data fabric. This unified approach allows companies to query and analyze data holistically, breaking down traditional data silos. The semantic linking of data creates a unified view, allowing for accurate contextual understanding of complex concepts, such as the term "material" in different scenarios like manufacturing, sales, or procurement.
  3. Improved Decision Making & Logical Inference: Knowledge graphs in general contribute to better data governance by establishing clearer relationships and rules for data entities, which improves logical reasoning and helps users make more informed, accurate decisions with fewer errors.

Conclusion:

With the addition of such advanced capabilities, SAP HANA Cloud continues to be the database of choice for developers and organizations, supporting critical applications within the SAP Business Technology Platform ecosystem. To stay tuned for further updates in this area, follow me on the SAP Community for regular insights, and check out Thomas Hammer’s What's New in SAP HANA Cloud blog series for the latest developments.

entry.title: A Sneak Peek into the Upcoming Dynamic Elasticity (Elastic Compute Node) in SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Already featured on the SAP Road Map Explorer and announced at SAP TechEd, the upcoming SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database QRC 04/2024 release will introduce Elastic Compute Node (ECN), a new dynamic elasticity feature.

As the name indicates, ECN is a read-only compute node (computeserver) that can be easily provisioned and deprovisioned based on incoming workloads or customer demands. With ECN, customers can efficiently address expected peak workloads, such as heavy planning jobs and Black Friday, while optimizing the size of the SAP HANA database in SAP HANA Cloud based on average workloads because ECNs can be spun up only when needed and then removed.

ECN has already been successfully used by SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) and SAP Datasphere for the purpose of addressing peak workloads while optimizing TCO, and now, it will be available for SAP HANA Cloud standalone use cases from QRC 04/2024. If you want to know more about how IBP benefits from the gained elasticity, then have a look at this blogpost and learn about IBP’s journey to SAP HANA Cloud.

Please find a nutshell of this new capability below:

  • A read-only compute node where the workload can be distributed.
  • Guided elasticity via REST API, btp Command Line Interface (CLI), or cf (Cloud Foundry) CLI.
  • Basically, equivalent to the indexserver but no persistency to store data permanently.
  • Replica tables and temporary tables can be created and stored in ECNs to accelerate query performance. These tables are removed when the ECNs are deprovisioned.
  • Since ECNs are not included in High Availability (HA) or Disaster Recovery (DR), this means there are no replicas for ECNs themselves for the purpose of HA/DR, resulting in a smaller footprint and lower TCO.
  • Workload Class-based dynamic workload routing.

As illustrated in the diagram above, while a 16 vCPUs and 256 GB SAP HANA database is intended for average workloads, a provision of 8 vCPUs and 128 GB ECN can be made on-demand to address peak workloads and then deprovisioned as needed. Essentially, the size of the ECN can be larger than the main SAP HANA database in SAP HANA Cloud, and multiple ECNs can also be provisioned. However, detailed restrictions, such as the maximum size of the ECN and the maximum number of parallel ECNs, will be announced with QRC 04/2024.

In conclusion, the upcoming introduction of ECN in SAP HANA Cloud represents a significant step forward in dynamic elasticity. This new feature will allow for greater flexibility and efficiency in addressing peak workloads, offering a promising solution for optimizing the size of the SAP HANA database while enhancing overall performance. As this is just a sneak peek and SAP HANA Cloud is continuously evolving, we encourage you to stay tuned for the upcoming updates.

entry.title: The next step towards cost-effectiveness and scalability with SAP HANA Cloud Multitenancy
entry.content:
With the upcoming release at the end of 2024, SAP HANA Cloud will introduce an innovation that supports native multitenancy capabilities on database-level. It allows you to manage data for different customers in dedicated database tenants sharing the same SAP HANA Cloud instance. 

Before we get into the details of the new feature, let’s look at multitenancy, what it is and why it matters.

Introduction

Multitenancy is a common architecture pattern in cloud environments, where multiple clients share the same resources and services. Each client is also named as tenant. While sharing the same resources, tenants are independent and separated from each other. You can think of an apartment house where the apartments have their own keys and own interiors but using the same infrastructure, e.g. for water and power supply. This “multitenant model” is very cost-effective as the individual units do not need to build their own expensive infrastructure.

Figure 1 - Multitenant Application

In the context of cloud applications, one multitenant application serves multiple customers in a single software instance. These customers are mapped to “application tenants”, sharing the same computing resources and services of the application. Finally, the data of the individual application tenants must be stored in an underlying database layer in “database tenants” (see Figure 1).

Why does Multitenancy matter?

With the multitenancy approach a cloud application uses a lower footprint of computing resources, as you don’t need to provision a dedicated software instance per customer. The cloud application can also simply scale by adding additional application tenants for new onboarded customers. With the rise of SaaS applications, the multitenant architecture becomes very popular, due to the significantly lower TCO. However, the application layer cannot provide enterprise qualities like customer-specific encryption or tenant-level data recovery. Those qualities can only be added by the database layer that ensures the logical separation of customer data, even though they are physically integrated in the same instance and sharing the same computing resources. 

The new SAP HANA Cloud Multitenancy capability will provide a modern data management layer for database tenants integrated in one database instance while keeping the balance between resource sharing and tenant separation. 

In the next section we will clarify some fundamental principles of SAP HANA Cloud database tenants.

What is a HANA Cloud database tenant?  

The SAP HANA Cloud database tenant is a first-class catalog object in the database that is associated with one or multiple database schemas. In other words, SAP HANA Cloud Multitenancy supports a schema-level multitenant model, where multiple database tenants are sharing the same SAP HANA Cloud instance and the same index server database process. This multitenant model provides a very scalable and cost-effective approach, starting from small up to large tenant sizes.

Since SAP HANA Cloud is a persistency service in the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), not only the SAP HANA Cloud database, but also each SAP HANA Cloud database tenant in the database is exposed as service instance to the SaaS multitenant applications. Whenever the application wants to persist data in an SAP HANA Cloud database tenant, it initially needs to create a database tenant as service instance and bind the corresponding application tenant or application service to the database tenant.

The SAP HANA Cloud database tenant itself is associated with one or multiple HDI-Containers/Schemas (see Figure 2). Both HDI-Containers and Schemas are also managed as service instances in BTP and can be added to or deleted from a database tenant at any time. 

Figure 2 - Database Tenant

To create database artefacts in HDI-Containers in a database tenant, you will use the Business Application Studio (BAS) that relies on HDI-Deployment. The BAS will connect directly to the HDI-Containers, as the database tenant association is transparent to it. For schemas, the application needs to create database artefacts via SQL-statements by using the database user and credentials of the schema.

How are SAP HANA Cloud database tenants managed?

As you can see in the center of Figure 3, there is a central Tenant Management Service (TMS) in BTP that provides all database tenant operations as REST APIs to the SaaS multitenant applications. The central TMS ensures that all HDI-Containers and schemas in a database tenant are handled consistently for tenant lifecycle operations.

In future, these TMS REST APIs can be consumed as CAP multitenancy services being part of the MTX package (MTX stands for multitenancy, feature toggle, and extensibility).

Figure 3 - HANA Cloud Database Tenants in the Business Technology Platform

Let’s look at the characteristics and planned features for SAP HANA Cloud database tenants in the next section.

Characteristics and Planned Features
  1. Tenant Encryption
    Each database tenant is encrypted with its own customer key by default. The customer key can be either managed by SAP (customer-specific encryption key) or managed by the customer (customer-managed key).

  2. Tenant Recovery
    You can recover each database tenant individually to any given point in time (PITR) within the defined backup retention time of the HANA Cloud instance.

  3. Tenant Move
    Database tenants can be moved between SAP HANA Cloud instances within the same datacenters. The feature will evolve towards an online tenant move (with near zero downtime) within and across datacenters. The feature will allow you e.g. to re-balance the number of managed tenants between SAP HANA Cloud instances or to relocate customer data.

  4. Tenant Data Copy
    To setup SAP HANA Cloudinstances with database tenants for test, training, or demo purposes, you can use the Tenant Data Copy feature. It allows you to copy tenant data between SAP HANA Cloudinstances. In its final version, the feature is also planned to allow a tenant copy across datacenters.

  5. Tenant Workload Management
    This feature avoids the so-called “noisy neighbor” effect, where database tenants may tend to claim computing resources as much as they need while other database tenants are suffering from lacking those resources. With tenant workload management you can assign workload classes with defined resource consumption limits to database tenants.

  6. Tenant Administration
    In the SAP HANA Cloud Central tooling you will get UI-based support to create, delete, recover database tenants, add, or remove HDI-Containers/schemas to database tenants as well as to monitor critical metrics of database tenants. Future development will include automation and mass-administration for hundreds or thousands of database tenants.

  7. Tenant Migration
    Coming from a HDI-Container or schema approach for multitenancy without isolation and recovery, cloud applications will migrate them to directly to database tenants leveraging all the new innovative features that are provided by SAP HANA Cloud.

Please note that not all of the planned features for SAP HANA Cloud Multitenancy will be available with the initial GA release by end of 2024. It is planned to deliver them gradually. Please follow the release information for multitenancy in the SAP HANA Cloud online help "What's New" section.

Conclusion

You can now benefit from the cost-effectiveness of SAP HANA Cloud Multitenancy, serving multiple customers while keeping their data logically separated within the same SAP HANA Cloud instance. Customers’ data is persisted in database tenants and encrypted with customer-specific keys. You can recover database tenants individually, without affecting other customers. You can scale your SAP HANA Cloud instance easily, starting with small database tenant sizes in a kilobyte range and growing as customer data requires. You can dynamically onboard new customers by adding new database tenants to the SAP HANA Cloud instance. And finally, you may use capabilities to gain fine-granular resource control and workload isolation for database tenants.

entry.title: Implico's Strategic Approach to Bottled Gas
entry.content:
Background: An Introduction to the Bottled Gas Industry

In the oil and gas industry, the bottled gas process involves the production, storage, transportation, and distribution of gases stored in portable containers like cylinders or tanks, serving various industrial, commercial, and residential applications. 

Many companies operate diversified gas distribution units, engaging in both bulk and bottled gas distribution. This dual approach enables them to cater to a broader range of customers and markets, leveraging their infrastructure, logistics, and expertise in handling both forms of gas. 

Implementing a system that effectively manages both bulk and bottled gas processes is essential for these companies. By integrating such a system, businesses streamline operations and enhance user experience, facilitating efficient implementation and usage. 

 

Benefits of Bottled Gas Integration in S4SCSD Solution: 

With no other system integrated into S4SCSD, Implico's offering of bulk and bottled gas solutions presents a significant opportunity for market expansion and revenue growth.  

 

1. Comprehensive Solution  

By providing bulk and bottled gas solutions within one system, Implico offers a comprehensive portfolio that meets the diverse needs of gas distribution companies,  

 

2. Integrated Solutions 

Having bulk and bottled gas solutions readily available on S4SCSD opens doors to the market, as many companies operate in both segments. 

 

3. Cost-Effective Management 

Offering a unified system for managing bulk and bottled gas operations translates into financial benefits for customers, reducing implementation costs compared to integrating multiple systems. 

 

4. Exclusive Gas Solutions 

Implico's status as the sole provider of integrated bulk and bottled gas solutions within S4SCSD offers a unique opportunity to increase revenue streams and eliminate revenue sharing with other suppliers. 

 

5. Innovate, Enhance, Differentiate 

Investing in innovation and enhancing bulk and bottled gas solutions in S4SCSD will differentiate Implico in the market, meeting evolving customer needs and establishing itself as a reliable partner for gas distribution management. 

 

Workflow 

A general scenario has been developed, starting with the creation of the sales order, passing through the dispatching tool (IDM) to standard data reconciliation (PAT), considering the return of empty and/or damaged bottles, which is a common practice in this type of business. Thus, Implico's bottled gas scenario enhances the already existing SDM solution by ensuring that the entire downstream logistic process for bulk material and bottled gas is covered by one tool. 

Important Features within the Process: 

  • Delivery Trip Planning: Ability to plan the delivery trips based on the special characteristics of the truck, such as the number of cages used to organize the cylinders in the truck, etc. 
  • Return Process: Efficient handling of the return process for full, empty as well as defective bottles.
  • Bottle Management: Handling of different sizes of bottles and automatic empty bottles return to stock at the end of each trip. 

 

Final Implementation 

The bottled gas scenario is a new functionality within the SDM solution and will be fully integrated into the existing processes. This ensures that customers have both bulk and bottled gas business processes consolidated into one solution, providing a comprehensive overview. 

With this solution, we support the business model of a full energy service provider for fuels and gas. 

 

Conclusion 

In conclusion, the integration of bulk and bottled gas solutions into S4SCSD represents a strategic opportunity for Implico to expand its market presence, drive revenue growth, and solidify its position as a leading provider of integrated solutions in the oil and gas industry. 

Read more about Bottled Gas here: https://www.implico.com/bottled-gas/

Let's shape a sustainable future, together.

 

SAP PAT: Implico’s Data Collation, especially its Process Automation Toolset (PAT), has revolutionized the way energy companies manage their data reconciliation and settlement processes. Developed by Implico and natively integrated with SAP S4SCSD, PAT uses cutting-edge Robotic Process Automation (RPA) technology to automate repetitive and manual tasks across multiple business functions.

SAP IDM: Integrated Dispatch Management (IDM) by Implico is a robust solution designed to enhance these capabilities within SAP S4SCSD. This advanced functionality is tailored for industries such as oil, gas and chemicals, where efficient logistics and environmental compliance are of paramount importance.

 

SAP S/4HANA SDM: This tool offers a cohesive approach to the trade and transport of liquids and half-solids, integrating seamlessly with the SAP system to streamline processes from demand forecasting to invoicing, thereby facilitating smoother operations in hydrocarbon product trading.

SAP S/4HANA RFNO: A dedicated solution for managing service station networks, it caters to various service station types, offering functionalities like wet goods management and loyalty card management, aiming to enhance the fuel retail experience.

Implico’s Quality Assurance Program for SAP solutions: Implico wants to ensure the implementation of SAP SDM/RFNO solutions is professional and error-free – not only when doing so ourselves but also when external companies are contracted to oversee implementation. This is why we also offer two partner services that are designed to supplement the implementation projects based on a methodological approach and with highly skilled consultants, both of which help to achieve the defined targets: the Partner Governance Service and the Partner Advisor Service.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic programming fail: pass table TYPE cds as a parameter
entry.content:
Hello Ulrich,

the problem is in the compatibility and I hope it could be only due to incorrect syntax:
 "The type of "I_T_DATA_OBS" cannot be converted to the type of "ME->T_ITEMS""

In the calling program, I have the i_t_data parameter declared as: t_data_obs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zfi_data_obs, which should do the job. I tried explicit typing, too, with no effect. ZFI_DATA_OBS is a CDS view entity.

The constructor is importing the parameter as: i_t_data_obs TYPE ANY TABLE.

The receiving object in the class's private attributes is declared as: t_items TYPE REF TO data (basically the only option, which does not trigger a syntax error).

Any ideas or recommendations, please?

Thank you.

Denis

entry.title: Re: Customized rating field in the performance form
entry.content:
Hello,

Whatever the final case, as design, the performance form will multiply the weight with goal rating. So you need to have a rating without weight multiplication. What you can do in that case is that you can have rating from manager and divide it by 100 in the goal plan xml, then have the actual-achievement as item rating/100. After that it will be automatically multiplied with item weight.

Have a nice day,

entry.title: (Re)Discover the Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators from SAP with our new Elearning
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

Did you know that SAP’s Out-of-the-Box Business Key Figures, now referred to as Process Performance Indicators (PPIs), have been available for over 15 years? Since their introduction, these PPIs have become fundamental to a wide range of process performance analysis tools for SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA. They played a critical role in tools such as the SAP Solution Manager-based SAP Business Process Improvement for SAP Solutions and Business Process Monitoring, and the SAP Innovation and Optimization tools. Now, it is also one of the key pillars of SAP Signavio Process Insights.

We are happy to share that we released a new e-learning course designed to guide you through the capabilities of SAP’s PPIs and the tools available within your SAP ES maintenance that will allow you to harness their potential to maximize your business’s efficiency.

Here’s what you can expect from the course: 

  1. Get an overview of SAP’s PPIs. Learn what Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators are, and understand how they provide immediate insights into business processes.
  2. Discover the powerful process analysis tools that come as part of your existing SAP maintenance contract and how you can leverage them to continuously optimize business processes.
  3. Learn how you can easily get started with the tools and apply the PPIs to your specific business process improvement goals.

Access the E-Learning:

Leveraging the Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators to Improve Business Processes

Requirement: Registration for the SAP Learning Hub is required to utilize e-learning sessions.  If you are not currently registered, please do so here: SAP Enterprise Support Academy

Join us in the Value Maps: 

If you are not yet signed up for Value Maps, please join via this link.

You will discover new opportunities to understand and analyze the bottlenecks in business processes and improve the identified challenges. The Business Process Transformation value map helps you to innovate your business processes in an ever-changing world. The SAP ES Value Maps require no additional costs to access for our SAP Enterprise Support and SAP PSLE customers.

Please don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation" topic).

entry.title: Re: Dynamic programming fail: pass table TYPE cds as a parameter
entry.content:
You are right, Sandra. Frustrated in the middle of a night, I did not consider this.

I get error: "The type of "I_T_DATA_OBS" cannot be converted to the type of "ME->T_ITEMS" and "T_ITEMS is not an internal table".

I expect: pass atable  structure corresponding to the structure of a CDS view entity as a parameter, so that I can populate it with data of that CDS in the receiving class.

I tried: declaring t_data_obs in the calling program as TYPE STANDARD TABLE, as table, and explicitely. I tried, desperate, also this in the receiving class:

*  CREATE DATA t_items LIKE i_t_data_obs.
*  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_items> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.
*  ASSIGN t_items->* TO <fs_items>.
*  <fs_items> = i_t_data_obs.

I assume that the problem lies in declaring the receiving structure as a reference to data in the private attributes:

t_items TYPE REF TO data,

and I hope someone can give me a recommendation for the correct declaration.

Regards,

Denis

entry.title: Does SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation require a special licenses or agreement?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have activated SAP Cloud ALM for monitoring (Operations). We would like to extend the same for Project creation and Transport management (Implementation). 

Is there any additional license required for Project configuration and Transport management system configuration. If license required, then what kind for license is require. Pls clarify.

 

Regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Does SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation require a speci...
entry.content:
Any Customer number that is entitled to an SAP Cloud ALM tenant is able to make use of its full suite of functionality without any additional requirements. This includes both the Operations functionality (monitoring, metrics, alerting, etc.) and the Implementation functionality (Project Management, Feature deployment, Testing, etc.). No additional licenses are required to make use of specific functionality of SAP Cloud ALM. 

Full steps to configure SAP Cloud ALM and its integrations can be found in the Setup and Administration for SAP Cloud ALM guide. More detailed information about how to actually use the functionality (split into different categories, such as Operations, Implementation, etc.) can be found in the SAP Cloud ALM Application Help.

entry.title: Re: BEHAVIOR IN STATUS PROFILES IN SALES VA02:
entry.content:
Hello @carlosgomezp 

You are right - my set up works unless you reach READ then go back to DRAF and want to go to CANC. Here the set up breaks. That is because the status management component "memorizes" the highest status. So if you reach READ, its status number 10 is memorized. So even if you get back from READ to DRAF, you can't go from DRAF to CANC, even if apparently the set up should allow that. 

That bug/feature is explained in the note 2047781 - BS042 or BS043 Error when status change is made. You can change that logic globally with modification note 159553 - Error messages BS042,BS043 or use the BAdI enhancement BSV_STATUS_PROFILE to implement your own validation logic. The BAdI is described in the note 1627673 - BAdI for status profile evaluation

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
entry.content:
Latest Innovations in a Nutshell

Continuing the blog series about updates on MDG innovations in SAP S/4HANA, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 02. Highlights include:

  • Generative AI for assisted changes/summary of changes in central governance (classic mode) and for business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) using Joule AI copilot.
  • Improved data quality analytics for business partner and product master data.
  • Enrichment of business partner from CDQ reference data​ as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

These come in addition to various continuous improvements in central governance (classic mode).

Innovations in Detail

Let's take a closer look at the innovations.

Inclusion of Generative AI in MDG

Generative AI is used for summary of changes and assisted changes in central governance (classic mode), and for optional business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) initiated by a Joule dialog. 

The AI-generated summary of changes provides you with an overview of changes in natural language (using a large language model) enabling you to: ​

  • verify the completeness and/or correctness of master data changes.​
  • get a brief overview of the changes comprised in an MDG change request.​
  • avoid pointless revisions based on incomplete changes.​
  • have a non-technical summary of changes (in contrast to change documents).​
  • facilitate approver’s final checks.

Generative AI-based summary of changes

​The AI-assisted change lets you change master data attributes with as little effort and as few steps as possible so that applying changes to a master data object is time efficient. You can make prompts in natural language to make field changes.

The AI-assisted change in central governance is applicable for business partner and material, and the AI-generated summary of changes in central governance is applicable for business partner, material, and custom objects which do not use editions.

In addition, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA integrates with the AI copilot Joule, enabling queries in natural language and navigation to SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA cloud-ready mode for follow-up actions on business partner (incl. supplier and customer) data. (This innovation can be consumed as of FPS01).

Joule integration with follow-ups in MDG (cloud-ready mode)

These initial steps of including Generative AI in MDG processes are planned to be continued to cover more use cases in MDG classic mode and cloud-ready mode. 

"Data Quality Analytics 2.0" for business partner and product master data

  • SAP Analytics Cloud comes with an enhanced dashboard for chief data officers or master data specialists to get a real-time quality abstract on the prevailing quality and history trends of product and business partner master data, providing:
    • data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure by category, dimension, and rule
    • data quality analytics for seven business partner segments, e.g., Business Partner Core Data, Customer Sales Data, and Supplier Purchasing Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Country, Company Codes etc.
    • data quality analytics for seven product master data segments, e.g., Plant data, Sales Data, and Classification Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Product Group, Plant etc.
    • embedded authorization control via live data connection
    • integration in MDG system for data remediation

Data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure: by category, dimension, and ruleDeep-dive data quality analytics on business partner in SAP Analytics Cloud

For more details about "Data Quality Analytics 2.0", read the expert blog post by Kefeng Wang.

  • Analytics CDS views for master data quality of business partner and product provide data source (CDS views) for master data quality analytics of business partner and product using organizational unit and attributes in SAP S/4HANA.

Enrichment of business partner records from reference data as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

In cloud-ready mode, the enrichment of business partner records from DDQ reference data is enabled as an integrated and automated step within:

  • Single Processing​
  • Mass Processing​
  • Consolidation​
  • Federation processes

Mass enrichment of business partners with reference data in SAP S/4HANA

For more information about the enrichment of business partner records from reference data with full process integration, read the SAP Community blog post by Martin Steinhauser.

Integration of SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

As an alternative to BTP Workflow Service (subscription required), the SAP Business Workflow can be assigned to process steps in the process template configuration. It can trigger any custom-defined action after a process step (e.g. email notification).

Integrate SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (material data)

Increased effectiveness through enhanced copy function in search and use of validation rules for classification in central governance for material: 

  • You can copy a selected material from the Search Materials Result List and choose one of three different copying options: Only Basic Data, All Data, or By Organizational Levels. When selecting Only Basic Data, basic data, long texts, and classification are copied. Choosing All Data will result in the complete data from the template material being copied. If you select By Organization Level, a dialog box will appear allowing you to choose which organizational data should be copied.​ 

Copy function in material search
Additionally, if a change request exists for the template material, the changed data from staging will be included in the copied material.

  • You can use validation rules for classes (table KSSK) and characteristics (table AUSP), defined using the rule management functionality of MDG, data quality management to check data during change request processing.

Validation rules for classes and characteristics

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (business partner incl. customer and supplier data)

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 comes with improved Partner Functions content display and relationship categories in business partner processing:

  • The relationship type allows you to classify the relationship category into different subdivisions. Accordingly, you can maintain the relationship type on the user interface for Relationships, Contact Persons, Shareholder Relationships, and their related details user interface.
  • In addition, there are more search criteria when using the F4 Help on the Partner field in the partner functions lists ERP Customer Sales Areas: Partner Functions or ERP Vendor Purchasing Organization: Partner Functions. The F4 help also includes Business Partner, Customer, Supplier and Maximum No. of Hits as search criteria. The UIBBs for Partner Functions include the column Assigned BP. 

UIBB Partner Functions

Maintaining the Relation Type

As you can see from the outlined innovations, both classic mode in MDG and cloud-ready mode are continuously enhanced. The driver for innovations in classic mode is primarily the SAP Continuous Influence program, whereas cloud-ready mode in MDG is the strategic development path going forward.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio up to 2025, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: SAP S/4 Hana - Zero Downtime Option
entry.content:
I am working on Release Upgrade from S/4 Hana 2021 to SAP S/4 Hana 2023, I’ve been diving into the Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) and wanted to make it easier for everyone to understand this game-changing approach for software updates.

Why ZDO?

System downtime during updates can be costly and disruptive. Imagine upgrading your system without halting operations—ZDO makes that possible! It allows updates for SAP S/4 HANA without technical downtime by keeping users working seamlessly while the update happens in the background.

How ZDO Works?

  1. Up-time Processing starts with your original system (Release 1). Users work as usual.
  2. Once ZDO is initiated, users transition smoothly to a bridge subsystem, which mimics the source system while the update is prepared in the background.
  3. Transactional Consistency is maintained throughout the process, ensuring your data stays intact.
  4. The target release (Release 2) is then activated, with minimal disruption. After a short restart (just 5-15 minutes), users are moved to the new release without them even realising it.
    Key Benefit
  • Minimal downtime: A single restart, no database reboot.
  • Low resource footprint: Only necessary tables are cloned.
  • No extra licenses or tools required.

ZDO not only reduces downtime but also helps keep business operations flowing seamlessly, ensuring system availability while upgrades happen in parallel

#sap #sapcommunity #SAPS/4HanaUpgrades #S/4HANA2021TO2023 #S4Hana @#S/4Hana

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content: Hi Dilip, The behavior to sync for MSA(using dump marker) and WSB is quite similar, ie there both exists enable marker. For MSA, an enable marker will first be sent out, following a dump marker. If you choose resync with "dump marker" for MSA, you have to purge the queue first. The existing data will still be there , and it will cause failure. That's also the benefit of using "resync marker" instead of "dump marker", as with resync marker, it will save the effort of manually cleaning up the queue. Hope this is clear now. Cheers Tony

entry.title: Re: Initial value of 0FISCPER is replaced wrongly
entry.content:
Hi Peter

0FISCPER is of type NUMC. The initial value for NUMC is 0. Since 0FISCPER is a NUMC with a length of 7, the initial value is 0000000.

BW did not allowed the usage of # on it's own as a characteristic value, as # represents the type corresponding initial value of a characteristic. So # is only used as a placeholder/mask to display the initial values in queries.

173241-Permitted characters in BW

===================================================

The following characteristic values can never be permitted:

  • The value that consists only of the single character '#' is not permitted because the initial input is masked with '#' for variables.
  • Values starting with the character '!' are not permitted because the variable entry is deleted at the first position due to this character.
  • Values that contain characters that contain the hexadecimal values HEX00 to HEX1F.

===================================================

entry.title: Error while testing SNMP TRAP from Solution Manager: "can't exec external program (2)" and "Exter
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP Solution Manager with ServiceNow via SNMP traps is a common practice to automate incident management and streamline IT operations. While testing SNMP traps, you might encounter the error messages "can't exec external program (2)" and "External program terminated with exit code 1."

Error Screenshot:

Troubleshooting Steps:

Make sure trapsend binary is in the $DIR_EXE directory of the instance and the <sidadm> user can start it

set the environment variable NLSUI_7BIT_FALLBACK to run the program

Execute the command ./trapsend -V

From the above screenshot we could understand the the Program is compiled with ICU version 65.1 of libicuu* library files. Current exe directory does not have the required version of the library files.

Solution:

Download the latest kernel file SAP KERNEL 7.93 64-BIT UNICODE PL 101 and copy the required libicuu* library files and place in the $DIR_EXE directory of the instance.

Required result:

Connection established
